pr.title: fix: redirect @icewhale/icewhale-files-openapi to @yundera mirror in Dockerfile build
pr.body:
## Problem

The package `@icewhale/icewhale-files-openapi` has been completely removed from npm, causing the `CasaOS-UI` build step in the Dockerfile to fail with:

```
ERR_PNPM_FETCH_404  GET https://registry.npmjs.org/@icewhale/icewhale-files-openapi/-/icewhale-files-openapi-1.3.0-alpha133.tgz: Not Found - 404
```

The `pnpm-lock.yaml` in the `CasaOS-UI` submodule (which uses `--frozen-lockfile`) pins the package to version `1.3.0-alpha133`, which no longer exists on npm.

## Solution

A mirror of the package exists on npm as `@yundera/icewhale-files-openapi@1.3.0-alpha133` (same version, same content, same code).

This PR adds a small build-time patch script (`build/patch-casaos-ui.cjs`) that runs in the Dockerfile before `pnpm install` to:

1. Update `package.json` to use pnpm's npm-alias syntax: `"npm:@yundera/icewhale-files-openapi@1.3.0-alpha133"` — so the package is still accessible in source code as `@icewhale/icewhale-files-openapi`.
2. Update the three corresponding entries in `pnpm-lock.yaml`:
   - Importer entry: specifier and version reference
   - Packages entry: package key and integrity hash
   - Snapshots entry: package key

`--frozen-lockfile` is preserved, ensuring all other dependencies remain at their exact locked versions.

## Changes

- `build/patch-casaos-ui.cjs` — new patch script
- `Dockerfile` — copy and run the patch script before `pnpm install`
pr.author: Copilot
